inline_comments.message: this is probably very application (wink) specific (application/* etc) - can you just add application/* and I am almost certain that if the application/README.md is explicitly added to git repo it will not be skipped - that is easy to test - and I did and it works - so everything what we want inside application folder can be added explicitly - this is intended to be empty and populated by user

Yes it will work; for files added to a path that is now ignored they are still tracked in index; for new files in ignored paths one just has to add "-f" cmd opt to "git add":

$ git add ansible/application/dummyfile
The following paths are ignored by one of your .gitignore files:
ansible/application/dummyfile
Use -f if you really want to add them.
$ git add -f ansible/application/dummyfile
$
